channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Ireland’s ‘George Floyd moment’ sparks protests demanding justice for Congolese man
item.description: The death of a Congolese-born man shortly after he was restrained by security guards outside a Dublin department store has sparked calls for justice in Ireland. Video clips show Yves Sakila unresponsive after being pinned to the ground as bystanders watched, drawing parallels to the 2020 killing of George Floyd by Minneapolis police.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Ex-reality TV star Spencer Pratt is 'running Trump's playbook' in LA mayoral race
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, May 22: Some 13 candidates are vying to defeat Karen Bass next month for the Los Angeles mayorship. Among them is former reality TV villain Spencer Pratt, whose social media savvy and debate skills have garnered support. Also, the controversial pro-doping Enhanced Games begin this weekend in Las Vegas. Plus the satirical parody Cockroach Janta political party in India becomes a viral sensation.
item.dc:creator: Dheepthika LAURENT

item.title: Paris: Far-right blamed as football fans riot
item.description: Six people were injured, one seriously, after around 100 supporters of Nice football club rioted in Paris on Thursday night ahead of the club’s Coupe de France final clash with Lens on Friday. Paris mayor Emmanuel Grégoire blamed the violence on “far-right” elements among Nice supporters.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pep Guardiola to step down as Manchester City manager after trophy-laden decade
item.description: Pep Guardiola will ​leave Manchester City at the end of ​the season after 10 years at the helm, the club announced on Friday, bringing an end to one ​of ‌the most successful and influential managerial ‌reigns in Premier League history.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Germany becomes destination of choice for Jews leaving Israel
item.description: Last year, for the first time in its history, Israel recorded a negative migration balance. In 2024, 82,700 Israelis left the country, an all-time record. In Europe, Germany is benefiting the most, attracting 18 percent of this emigration according to the OECD. Settling in Germany is facilitated by reparation laws that give descendants of Holocaust victims easier access to German nationality. Since 2022, passport applications have surged, despite a rise in anti-Semitism in the country. FRANCE 24's Anne Mailliet, Kilian-Davy Baujard and Willy Mahler report.
item.dc:creator: Anne MAILLIET

item.title: Trump says sending 5,000 US troops to Poland
item.description: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he was sending 5,000 troops to Poland, after Washington said an earlier planned deployment had been called off amid pressure on Europe to fend for itself. Trump said the move was based on his relationship with Poland's president Karol Nawrocki, a nationalist ally whom he said he was "proud to endorse" in elections that Nawrocki won last year.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Mali-Guinea corridor disrupted: Militants siege deepens economic strain
item.description: Following the coordinated attacks launched in late April, security conditions have sharply deteriorated along the key transport corridor connecting Mali and Guinea. Reports of a blockade near the Bamako region have brought much of the road traffic to a halt, severely affecting the movement of people and goods ahead of the Tabaski holiday. The disruption has left drivers stranded, traders unable to transport merchandise, and local economic activity slowing across the region. Many families also remain without news from relatives who departed along the affected routes.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: In Cape Town, women-led cookies business supply British Airways
item.description: In one of South Africa’s largest townships, a small bakery is turning cookies into jobs. Khayelitsha Cookies, a women-led business on the outskirts of Cape Town, has secured a deal to supply British Airways, a breakthrough the company says will create more employment for women in a community hit hard by joblessness.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ukraine hits Russian oil refinery, Zelensky says, as Kyiv intensifies new tactic
item.description: Ukrainian forces attacked an ​oil refinery in Russia's Yaroslavl region, President Volodymyr Zelensky said Friday, the ​latest ‌in a series of strikes ⁠by Kyiv on Russian oil facilities. Authorities in Ukraine's Russian-controlled Luhansk region on Friday said a Ukrainian drone attack on a student dormitory killed at least one person.
item.source: © AFP via Handout from the Russian-installed head of Luhansk region
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo finally wins his first major title with Al-Nassr
item.description: After three years in Saudi Arabia, Cristiano Ronaldo has finally lifted the Saudi Pro League trophy. The Portuguese forward scored twice in Al-Nassr's decisive victory against Damac on the final day of the Saudi league season. This win crowns Al-Nassr champions for the first time since 2019.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Paul McCartney helps Stephen Colbert say goodbye to 'Late Show'
item.description: Comedian Stephen Colbert signed off from his late-night talk show on Thursday after 11 seasons with a sentimental chat with Beatles musician Paul McCartney and pointed jokes about his forced departure from CBS. The final installment of "The Late Show with Stephen Colbert" began with the comic thanking the in-person and television audience for watching ​his nightly take on ‌current events, often punctuated by verbal jabs at Republican President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Greenlanders demonstrate against Trump as US diplomats open new consulate
item.description: The ⁠US opening of ​a larger diplomatic hub in Greenland on Thursday was met by demonstrators opposed to President ​Donald Trump's ambition for greater influence in the Arctic island, while local government ministers shied away from the event. Moving from a wooden cabin on the outskirts of Nuuk to ​a much larger ‌downtown office, the new United States consulate has become a ⁠focal point for Greenlanders unhappy with Trump's stated desire to control the island, a semi-autonomous part of Denmark.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Turkey political crisis simmers as opposition vows to resist court ruling
item.description: Turkey's political ​opposition dug in on Friday to resist an unprecedented court ruling that ousted its leader and annulled its congress, inflaming a political crisis that critics say aims to further prolong President Tayyip Erdogan's 23-year rule. The CHP condemned the ruling as a "judicial coup" and Ozel vowed to fight it through legal appeals and to personally remain "day and ‌night" in the main opposition party's headquarters in Ankara.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type
item.description: An Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has spread to eastern South Kivu province in an area under the control of the Rwanda-backed M23 militia, the group's spokesman said Thursday, heightening fears of the deadly disease's growing reach. Efforts to get a grip on the latest outbreak of the highly contagious haemorrhagic fever, which the World Health Organization has declared an international emergency, have been hampered by the DRC's long-running conflicts.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Hello, Goodbye': 'Late Show' host Stephen Colbert bows out
item.description: Paul McCartney led an all-star lineup for the final episode of "The Late Show" as frontman Stephen Colbert bowed out after broadcaster CBS cancelled his show as it courted US President Donald Trump. The show, which Colbert has hosted since 2015, was axed after he mocked the broadcaster for a $16 million settlement with Trump for allegedly "maliciously" editing an interview with his Democratic election rival Kamala Harris. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Lebanese accuse Israel of wiping their towns off the map
item.description: Lebanese mother-of-two Hala Farah is collecting photos and videos to preserve the memory of her hometown which, like many others along the southern border, has been completely destroyed by Israeli forces. Testimony from residents and officials, as well as satellite images and photographs taken by AFP journalists on both sides of the border, show widespread destruction in dozens of Lebanese towns and villages since the start of the Israel-Hezbollah war on March 2.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Paris police arrest brawling Nice fans ahead of French Cup final, relegation showdown
item.description: Dozens of people were detained overnight in the French capital after a violent brawl involving fans of OGC Nice, further blemishing a torrid season for the Riviera team who face Lens in the French Cup final on Friday and must win a two-legged play-off next week to remain in Ligue 1.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: US Navy official says Taiwan arm sales on 'pause' over Iran war
item.description: The acting US Navy secretary said Thursday that arm sales to Taiwan had been put on "pause" to ensure that the American military had sufficient munitions for its Iran operations. Asked at a congressional hearing about the stalled $14 billion weapons purchase by Taiwan, acting secretary Hung Cao said that "right now we're doing a pause in order to make sure we have the munitions we need for Epic Fury -- which we have plenty." Ahead of his recent state visit to China, Donald Trump said he would speak with Chinese President Xi Jinping about the arms sales, a departure from Washington's previous insistence that it will not consult Beijing on the matter.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Latvia, standing with Ukraine
item.description:
Baltic states, shaped by their Soviet past, are at the forefront of the conflict in Ukraine.
They are surrounded by Russia, Belarus and the Russian enclave of Kaliningrad. But these members of NATO and the European Union have been providing support to Ukrainians since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.
Valentine Patry and Hugo Laridon went to Latvia to meet the invisible heroes who stand for peace and solidarity and extend their hands to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Los Angeles World Cup workers demand ‘ICE out’ during tournament
item.description: Workers at Los Angeles's SoFi Stadium vowed Monday to go on strike if federal immigration enforcement agents are deployed at the venue when it hosts World Cup matches next month.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Late Show' host Colbert bows out with a little help from Paul McCartney, Trump exults
item.description: Former Beatles Paul McCartney led an all-star lineup for the final episode of "The Late Show" as host Stephen Colbert bowed out after broadcaster CBS cancelled his show as it courted US President Donald Trump. "Thank goodness he's finally gone," Trump said Friday in a social media post that derided the beloved American comedian's talent.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Curtains down: Stephen Colbert bows out of 'Late Show' after Trump pressure
item.description: Paul McCartney led an all-star lineup for the final episode of "The Late Show" as host Stephen Colbert bowed out after broadcaster CBS cancelled his show as it courted US President Donald Trump. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert notes that Colbert's exit could be seen as "evidence of Trump’s full-frontal assault on US democracy".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Cold, pragmatic relationship': UAE-Israel ties driven by defence and technology
item.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr Andreas Krieg, Senior Lecturer at the School of Security Studies at King's College London and Fellow at the Institute of Middle Eastern Studies. According to Krieg, "Iran is fighting a war against the global economy." By targeting the UAE, and especially Dubai, Tehran is not merely retaliating militarily but seeking to exploit what Krieg calls the "Achilles heel" of the international system: the Gulf's hyperconnected financial and logistical infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Nadia MASSIH

item.title: Alberta to hold October referendum on whether to remain in Canada
item.description: Canada's oil-producing province of Alberta will hold an October referendum on separation from Canada, but Premier Danielle Smith said on Thursday the vote would not directly decide independence. Instead, voters will be asked whether the province should take constitutional steps towards a future binding referendum on leaving the federation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
